//: Config for the /feature-map skill. Copy this whole .claude/ folder into your project, then edit this file. Paths are RELATIVE to the wiki repo root so they travel across machines/clones. A filled-in microservices example is in examples/hark/.
_topology_note: monolith | microservices. monolith ⇒ file→capability tags only (the shared compiler + test suite are the cross-module propagation net). microservices ⇒ also a service partition + cross-service contracts.
_capabilitySource_note: graph-tags = derive a small controlled capability vocab from the graph's per-file tags (seed with: node .claude/skills/feature-map/query-graph.mjs node-tags <graph.json>). subsystem-pages = the wiki is already code-shaped (one page per module), reuse the subsystem slugs as capability tags.
_featureSources_note: Authoritative sources of user-facing features + their status (status docs, user stories, roadmap, README, backlog). The feature-mapper agent reads these to draft the feature register.
_services_note: (microservices only) graph layers grouped into deployable/process units: { name, language, layers:[<graph layer names>] }. See examples/hark for a polyglot (Swift + TS) example. Different languages ⇒ no shared compiler ⇒ cross-service contracts matter most.
_contracts_note: (microservices only) the un-greppable cross-service edges: { name, between:[svcA,svcB], files:[<seam files>], checkedBy:[<contract tests>], note }. Wire protocols, API/event schemas (OpenAPI/proto/GraphQL), IPC bridges, deploy/compose manifests. Source from the contract files, NOT the code graph (services don't import each other).
